item.title: KGIRS Boss Urges Youths To Embrace Innovation, Entrepreneurship
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The executive chairman of the Kogi State Internal Revenue Service (KGIRS), Dr Sule Salihu Enehe, has urged Nigerian youths to embrace knowledge, innovation and entrepreneurship as pathways to self-reliance, saying the future belongs to young people equipped with digital skills and creative thinking.

item.title: Kano: Lawyer regains freedom after 21 days in captivity
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Barrister Hashim Abdullahi has regained his freedom after spending 21 days in captivity following his abduction while travelling to Sokoto. Sources said the lawyer was released after his family and well-wishers reportedly paid an unspecified ransom to secure his freedom. Barrister Abdullahi was kidn

item.title: Is SpaceX’s stock a bust because it fell below $135? Look what happened after Meta’s IPO.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Close to half of major IPOs sink below their offering price — and stay there for several years. Here’s why SpaceX is no different.

item.title: Jonathan condoles with Amaechi over mother’s passing
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Former President Dr. Goodluck Ebele Jonathan has expressed sadness over the passing of Dame Ezinne Mary Amaechi, mother of former Governor of Rivers State and African Democratic Congress (ADC) vice presidential candidate, Rt. Hon. Chibuike Rotimi Amaechi.

item.title: Cucurella: I’ll Retire, Get A Tattoo If Spain Win World Cup 2026
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: New Real Madrid signing Marc Cucurella has pledged that if Spain wins the World Cup 2026, he will get a tattoo of manager Luis de la Fuente’s face on his body. Cucurella made the bold vow leading up to the final against Argentina on Sunday. Read Also:World Cup 2026: Laporte Warns Spain Of Arge

item.dc:creator: Daily Post

item.title: Josh Kerr smashes men’s mile world record that stood unbroken since 1999
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Britain's Josh Kerr clocked 3min 42.66sec in the men's mile at a high-octane Diamond League on Saturday, smashing the record set by Morocco's Hicham El Guerrouj in Rome in 1999.

item.description: The Chairman of the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission ( ICPC), Dr. Musa Adamu Aliyu, SAN, has outlined a 3-point plan to tackle corruption in Africa, saying it requires building strong institutions, nurturing ethical leadership, and promoting a culture of integrity

item.title: Here’s the net worth you need to start getting ahead in your 20s, 30s, 40s and beyond in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: About three-fourths of Americans lack the wealth needed to prosper in today’s economy, according to a new benchmark developed by the Aspen Institute.

item.title: C’River: 2.5M People Expected To Vote In 2027 – IPAC
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: An estimated 2.5 million eligible voters in Cross River State are expected to fulfill their civic duty of voting for any party of their choice in the 2027 election, according to the projection of the leàdership of Inter-Party Advisory Council (IPAC). The council, which made this disclosure on Saturd

item.title: Will the World Cup affect Arsenal's transfer plans?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: With the World Cup final taking place on Sunday and the start of the Premier League just over a month away, supporters are starting to think about how their side is shaping up for the new campaign.

item.title: World Cup Third-Place Play-Off Should Be Scrapped —Shearer
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Alan Shearer has branded FIFA’s third-place play-off ‘meaningless’ ahead of England’s meeting with France this evening. England’s defeat by Argentina in Wednesday’s semi-final ended their hopes of reaching Sunday’s World Cup final against Spain in New Jersey

item.title: Jigawa Launches Digital Tax Platform to Boost Revenue, End Leakages
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Jigawa State Government is taking another step to strengthen its economy with the launch of a digital tax platform aimed at improving revenue collection from the informal sector, ending leakages and making tax payments more transparent. The post Jigawa Launches Digital Tax Platform to Boost Reve

item.title: NCC, NDLEA Sign MoU To Combat Copyright Piracy, Organised Crime
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigerian Copyright Commission (NCC) and the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen inter-agency cooperation in combating copyright piracy and related organised criminal activities. The MoU was signed yesterday, Friday, July 17,

item.title: Tinubu’s reforms raised Imo FAAC allocation from N100m to N10.7bn, cut debt to below N90bn – Uzodimma
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Hope Uzodimma, the governor of Imo State, has credited the economic reforms of President Bola Tinubu for the sharp increase read more Tinubu’s reforms raised Imo FAAC allocation from N100m to N10.7bn, cut debt to below N90bn – Uzodimma

item.title: 2027: Gombe PDP guber aspirant withdraws Court case, endorses Pantami
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: One of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) governorship aspirants in Gombe State, Hon. Monica Yahaya Kaltho, has officially withdrawn from the court case instituted by some aggrieved, non-cleared governorship aspirants challenging the outcome of the party’s governorship primary election. It wou

item.title: AI may force these workers to retire earlier than planned
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Older workers now face two major impediments: looming Social Security cuts and rapidly evolving workplace priorities

item.title: N210b duplicate allocations: Atiku demands NASS’ probe of 2026 budget
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Atiku said the revelation, coming alongside Nigeria's poor showing on nearly 90 percent of globally recognised prosperity indicators, exposes the Tinubu administration as one of the most fiscally reckless governments in Nigeria's democratic history. The post N210b duplicate allocations: Atiku demand

item.title: ‘4 years not enough for him to finish Abuja work’: Tinubu, Shettima praise Wike
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: They spoke in Abuja on Friday at a dinner organised to celebrate the inauguration of 31 projects across the territory within a 28-day window The post ‘4 years not enough for him to finish Abuja work’: Tinubu, Shettima praise Wike appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Apo-Karshi road will be completed before end of 2026, Wike assures Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Minister, Nyesom Wike, has assured President Bola Ahmed Tinubu that the long-abandoned 13-kilometre Apo-Karshi Road will be completed before the end of this year, 2026. The minister gave the assurance while commissioning the reconstructed Arab/Gbazango Road in Kubwa,
